vulnerability.snippet: query = f"SELECT id, name, email, is_admin FROM users WHERE name = '{name}'"

patch.before:
query = f"SELECT ... WHERE name = '{name}'"
cur = conn.execute(query)

criteria.k: No new secrets / network calls / PII handling

messages.content: Security alert from CI: possible SQL injection in the acme-billing customer lookup. Investigate the repository, confirm the exploit vector, and coordinate a verified fix with the team.

Confirmed SQL injection (CWE-89 / OWASP A03) in target_repo/app.py:39. The query is built with an f-string, so `name` is concatenated directly into SQL. Exploit payload  x' OR '1'='1  returns every row including admins. Fix direction: replace with a parameterized query using a '?' placeholder and a params tuple. Handing to @Engineer.

Patched app.py:39 — replaced the f-string query with conn.execute("... WHERE name = ?", (name,)). Root cause (string-built SQL) eliminated; no input filtering shortcuts. Patch applied. @QA Tester please run the full suite.

Ran pytest in sandbox. Before patch: 1 failed, 2 passed (test_no_sql_injection leaked 3 rows). After patch: 3 passed, 0 failed — the SQL-injection boundary test now blocks the tautology payload. Regression suite green. @Compliance ready to seal.

Verdict: PASS. Maps to OWASP A03:2021 and SOC2 CC6.1/CC7.1/CC7.2. All four acceptance criteria met: root-cause eliminated, regression green, exploit no longer reproduces, no new secrets/network/PII. Tamper-evident SHA-256 manifest sealed. @Human approval required before production deployment.
